title: ormerodia sagittata
side_effects: Ormerodia sagittata can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal problems. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea. Additionally, improper use of Ormerodia sagittata may also cause respiratory issues, including shortness of breath and coughing. If not handled correctly, the plant's compounds can also cause eye irritation and temporary vision loss.
